name: Chorro de Navafría and Refugios Loop
name_original: Chorro de Navafría y Mirador de las Cebadillas — circular por el Refugio de la Fragua: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama
description: This moderate 7.0-mile hike in Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama features the Chorro de Navafría Waterfall and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Navafría offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, characterized by extensive pine forests and river valleys. The region features notable natural attractions such as the 20-meter high El Chorro Waterfall and the Cega River, which carves through the landscape. Trails often lead through the Pinar de Navafría and ascend to points like the Puerto de Navafría, providing varied elevation profiles. Best hiking trails around Navafría The most popular hiking route is Chorro de Navafría and Refugios Loop, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the impressive El Chorro Waterfall and local refuges. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto de Navafría – Chorro de Navafría Waterfall loop from Restaurante El Chorro, a difficult 12.7 miles (20.4 km) path. This challenging route connects the mountain pass with the iconic waterfall, offering varied terrain and ascents. Local hikers also love the Chorro de Navafría Waterfall – Chorro Waterfall loop from Restaurante El Chorro, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through the natural park area, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Navafría is defined by extensive pine forests, the El Chorro Waterfall, and the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain pass. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Navafría are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Navafría's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Navafría?
A: Navafría offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the extensive pine forests, river valleys, and mountain passes of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Navafría?
A: Yes, Navafría has several accessible options. For a shorter, family-friendly walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14883800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Navafría Waterfall – Chorro Waterfall loop from Centro Forestal de Majalcarro&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3 km. Another moderate option that allows you to experience the impressive El Chorro Waterfall is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14883792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Navafría Waterfall – Chorro Waterfall loop from Restaurante El Chorro&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.3 km trail.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Navafría?
A: Many trails in the Navafría area, particularly within the Pinar de Navafría and around the El Chorro Waterfall, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations within the Sierra de Guadarrama Natural Park or National Park sections.

Q: What are the main natural attractions I can see while hiking in Navafría?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. A highlight is the impressive 20-meter high &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt; (El Chorro Waterfall), a focal point for many routes. You'll also encounter the vast Pinar de Navafría, the scenic Río Cega, and the historic Martinete de Navafría. For panoramic views, routes often lead towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt; or even the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Navafría?
A: Yes, many of the popular routes in Navafría are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153100325/chorro-de-navafria-and-refugios-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Navafría and Refugios Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.3 km trail, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14884187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Navafría – Chorro de Navafría Waterfall loop from Restaurante El Chorro&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 20.4 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navafría?
A: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking in Navafría, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases beautiful fall colors in the pine forests. Summer can be warm, but the recreational area around El Chorro with its natural swimming pool provides a refreshing option. Winter offers opportunities for snowshoeing or cross-country skiing, especially around the Puerto de Navafría.

Q: How can I get to Navafría using public transport?
A: Navafría is accessible by bus from Segovia. There are also bus connections from Madrid to Segovia, from where you can take a local bus to Navafría. It's advisable to check the latest bus schedules as services may be less frequent on weekends or holidays.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a hike in Navafría?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads, especially around the El Chorro Waterfall recreational area and in the village of Navafría itself. For routes starting at higher elevations, such as those near the Puerto de Navafría, designated parking areas are usually provided.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navafría?
A: The hiking routes in Navafría are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the El Chorro Waterfall, the extensive pine forests, and the well-maintained paths that offer varied terrain for all abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Navafría?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14884187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Navafría – Chorro de Navafría Waterfall loop from Restaurante El Chorro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.4 km route with significant elevation gain. Additionally, trails leading to the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt; offer a strenuous climb with rewarding panoramic views.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see in the Navafría area?
A: Navafría is characterized by its diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll hike through extensive Scots pine forests (Pinar de Navafría), discover the dramatic El Chorro Waterfall, and follow the course of the Río Cega. The terrain ranges from gentle river valleys to the higher altitudes of mountain passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied scenery throughout your journey.

Q: Is there a natural swimming area near the trails?
A: Yes, the Parque Natural El Chorro, surrounding the El Chorro Waterfall, features a natural swimming pool fed by the pristine waters of the Cega River. It's a popular spot for relaxation and cooling off, especially during the warmer summer months, after completing a hike.
tips: Ideal to go with people you love ...
Viewpoint from where we can see beautiful views of the valley as a whole and which is also a good point to rest in a unique and quiet place.
Refuge that is located at the foot of the Río Cega and that is inside what are called the Montes Carpetanos.

faqs.question: Where does the Chorro de Navafría y Mirador de las Cebadillas trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts at the recreational area of El Chorro, near Navafría. This area is well-equipped with facilities. Yes, there is parking available at the trailhead, making it convenient for visitors arriving by car.

faqs.question: Is the Chorro de Navafría y Mirador de las Cebadillas trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is rated as moderate, requiring good fitness. However, a shorter, direct path specifically to the Chorro de Navafría Waterfall is considered easy to moderate and is very suitable for families and beginners. The initial section from the recreational area to the waterfall is particularly easy and short.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers diverse scenery. You'll encounter the impressive Chorro de Navafría Waterfall, which is particularly spectacular in spring. The Mirador de los Castillejos (also known as Mirador de las Cebadillas) provides stunning panoramic views of the Navafría pine forest and the Cega River valley. The route also winds through beautiful Scots pine forests and passes by several mountain refuges, including the La Fragua Refuge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but the Chorro de Navafría Waterfall is most impressive in late winter or springtime due to snowmelt and rainfall. Spring also brings lush vegetation, while autumn offers vibrant fall colors in the pine forests. Summer provides pleasant shade, and winter can be magical with snow, though conditions may be more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Sierra Norte de Guadarrama Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific restrictions, especially concerning fire bans or protected areas.

faqs.question: Is the Chorro de Navafría y Mirador de las Cebadillas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it is located within a Natural Park, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hiking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of experiences. You'll encounter unpaved tracks, paved sections, mountain trails, and natural paths. While mostly accessible, some areas require sure-footedness. The route often follows alongside the Arroyo del Chorro and the Cega River, providing the constant sound of water.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other paths. You will find it crosses parts of the Camino de Peñalara and the Pista esquí de fondo Navafría.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Norte de Guadarrama is rich in wildlife. Hikers on this trail might spot deer, wild boars, and squirrels. The area is also known for a wide variety of mushrooms, particularly in autumn.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting point at the recreational area of El Chorro is well-equipped. You'll find picnic tables, barbecue facilities, and even a natural swimming pool, making it a great spot for a day out before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. Sun protection, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit are also recommended.
